skills.name: accessibility
skills.description: Use when auditing, implementing, or reviewing web accessibility (WCAG, ARIA, keyboard navigation, screen reader support, and inclusive interaction patterns).

skills.name: agentic-eval
skills.description: Use when evaluating an agent, skill, workflow, or MCP server: rubric design, evaluator-optimizer loops, LLM-as-judge patterns, regression suites, or prototype-vs-production quality gaps.

skills.name: api-designer
skills.description: Use when defining or reviewing external API contracts, OpenAPI specifications, resource models, pagination, versioning, and error response standards. Do not use for pure database design or framework-only handler wiring.

skills.name: api-patterns
skills.description: Use when choosing between REST, GraphQL, and type-safe RPC patterns, or when standardizing response envelopes, versioning, pagination, auth shape, and API protection strategy.

skills.name: architecture-designer
skills.description: Use when designing or reviewing system architecture, interface boundaries, tradeoffs, ADRs, scalability posture, and the smallest durable target shape for a product or platform.

skills.name: auth-architect
skills.description: Use when designing or reviewing authentication and authorization for backend systems, including sessions, tokens, OAuth or OIDC, RBAC or ABAC, passkeys, service-to-service auth, and policy enforcement boundaries.

skills.name: c-pro
skills.description: Use for modern C23-era systems and embedded engineering with memory safety discipline, build reproducibility, and low-level debugging awareness.

skills.name: ci-cd-pipelines
skills.description: Use when designing, reviewing, or debugging CI/CD pipelines across GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, and similar platforms. Covers pipeline architecture, job sequencing, caching, artifact management, environment promotion, security hardening, and flaky-pipeline triage.

skills.name: cpp-pro
skills.description: Use for modern C++23-era systems and application engineering with RAII, value semantics, toolchain awareness, and performance-safe design.

skills.name: csharp-pro
skills.description: Use for modern C# backend/application engineering with C# 15 and .NET 11-era production practices.

skills.name: dart-pro
skills.description: Use for modern Dart engineering across Flutter, server, and CLI code with Dart 3.11-era language features, package hygiene, isolates, and testing discipline.

skills.name: database-design
skills.description: Use when designing schemas, relationships, indexes, ORM boundaries, and safe migration plans for relational or document-oriented workloads.

skills.name: database-optimizer
skills.description: Use for database performance triage across engines: query-plan analysis, indexing changes, wait and contention diagnosis, config tuning, and safe before/after validation.

skills.name: database-skills
skills.description: Use as the primary database hub for engine or platform choice, schema-vs-tuning routing, access-layer triage, migration safety framing, and database task triage before deeper engine, platform, or ORM specialists.

skills.name: debugging-strategies
skills.description: Use when isolating bugs, regressions, flaky tests, or cross-layer failures with an evidence-first reproduce, narrow, verify workflow.

skills.name: deep-research
skills.description: Use when a task needs multi-round research rather than a quick lookup: iterative search, gap finding, corroboration across sources, contradiction handling, or evidence-led synthesis before planning or implementation.

skills.name: design-system-builder
skills.description: Use when creating or reviewing reusable UI components, token-driven styling, variant APIs, theming hooks, and component documentation for a design system.

skills.name: docker-kubernetes
skills.description: Use for containerization strategy, Dockerfile optimization, Kubernetes deployment patterns, Helm charts, and container orchestration decisions.

skills.name: drizzle-expert
skills.description: Use when the task is specifically Drizzle ORM or drizzle-kit: schema-as-code, typed SQL, relations, migrations, config, runtime pairing, or TypeScript database access-layer decisions across Postgres, SQLite/libSQL, Neon, or Supabase.

skills.name: fastapi-expert
skills.description: Use when building FastAPI services with async Python, Pydantic v2, lifespan-managed resources, dependency-based auth, background task boundaries, and OpenAPI-safe request handling.

skills.name: firebase
skills.description: Use when the task is specifically Firebase: Firestore or Realtime Database choice, security rules, indexes, auth, storage, cloud functions, hosting, emulator workflows, or platform-coupled rollout decisions.

skills.name: flutter-design-system
skills.description: Use when creating, auditing, or extending shared Flutter theme tokens, ThemeData, and reusable cross-feature UI primitives such as loading, error, empty, dead-letter, and sync-status components. Do not use for one-off screen styling that should remain local.

skills.name: flutter-drift
skills.description: Use when implementing or refactoring Flutter local persistence with Drift: tables, DAOs, typed queries, indexes, transactions, and migrations. Do not use for simple key-value storage or remote-only networking work.

skills.name: flutter-feature
skills.description: Use when scaffolding a full Flutter feature across data, domain, presentation, routing, offline sync, and tests. Do not use for isolated single-layer work when a narrower Flutter skill already fits.

skills.name: flutter-go-router
skills.description: Use when adding or refactoring typed GoRouter navigation, guards, shell routes, deep links, or notification-driven routing in Flutter. Do not use for state management or feature business logic.

skills.name: flutter-offline-sync
skills.description: Use when implementing or auditing offline-first sync in Flutter with a local database, outbox queue, retry policy, conflict handling, and dead-letter behavior. Do not use for simple online-only CRUD.

skills.name: flutter-repository
skills.description: Use when building or refactoring a Flutter feature data layer: Drift tables and DAOs, DTOs, mappers, Retrofit APIs, and repository orchestration. Do not use when the task needs a full feature scaffold across presentation and routing.

skills.name: flutter-riverpod
skills.description: Use when creating, refactoring, or auditing Riverpod 3 providers and notifiers in Flutter with code generation and correct lifecycle boundaries. Do not use for widget-local ephemeral state that should stay inside the UI.

skills.name: flutter-state-machine
skills.description: Use when implementing or auditing the shared Flutter screen-state contract: loading, data, empty, error, and dead-letter rendering plus consistent error mapping. Do not use for generic Riverpod work when the project does not use this AppState pattern.

skills.name: flutter-testing
skills.description: Use when adding or refactoring Flutter tests for repositories, services, Riverpod notifiers, widgets, and golden coverage around a feature. Do not use for production feature implementation unless the task is explicitly test-focused.

skills.name: frontend-code-review
skills.description: Use when reviewing web UI code for regressions in accessibility, state flow, responsiveness, loading and error behavior, rendering cost, and design-system consistency.

skills.name: frontend-design
skills.description: Use for web UI design decisions: layout, hierarchy, typography, color, motion, and interaction clarity. This skill teaches decision-making, not a fixed visual style.

skills.name: game-development
skills.description: Use when implementing game mechanics, game loops, physics, AI, collision detection, or performance optimization for web, mobile, PC, or VR/AR games.

skills.name: geo-fundamentals
skills.description: Use when optimizing content for AI search engines (ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity) using Generative Engine Optimization techniques and citation-worthy content patterns.

skills.name: git-workflow
skills.description: Use when establishing or reviewing git branching strategies, PR conventions, commit hygiene, merge policies, monorepo workflows, and release tagging. Covers trunk-based development, GitFlow alternatives, conventional commits, and code review standards.

skills.name: golang-pro
skills.description: Use for modern Go services and tooling with Go 1.26-era language/runtime patterns, concurrency safety, and production operations.

skills.name: graphql-architect
skills.description: Use when designing GraphQL schemas, resolver boundaries, batching strategy, auth and policy enforcement, federation shape, query safety, and real-time graph behavior.

skills.name: java-pro
skills.description: Use for modern Java backend and platform engineering with Java 25-era language/runtime practices.

skills.name: javascript-pro
skills.description: Use for modern JavaScript architecture and implementation across browser, Node, and edge runtimes with runtime-aware production practices.

skills.name: kotlin-pro
skills.description: Use for modern Kotlin backend/mobile/shared code with Kotlin 2.3-era language tooling and production patterns.

skills.name: legacy-modernizer
skills.description: Use when planning and executing incremental migration of legacy systems using strangler fig, branch by abstraction, and feature-flag-controlled rollouts.

skills.name: mcp-builder
skills.description: Use when building, extending, or evaluating an MCP server: tool design, resource patterns, transports, auth, schemas, testing, Python or TypeScript implementation, or MCP-specific server architecture decisions.

skills.name: microservices-architect
skills.description: Use when decomposing systems into services, designing service boundaries, choosing sync and async integration patterns, and managing distributed-system reliability tradeoffs.

skills.name: mongodb
skills.description: Use when the task is specifically MongoDB: document-model design, aggregation pipelines, index strategy, shard-key tradeoffs, and operational guidance for document workloads.

skills.name: mysql
skills.description: Use when the task is specifically MySQL or InnoDB: schema/index design, transaction behavior, replication-aware migrations, query tuning, and managed MySQL operational tradeoffs.

skills.name: neki
skills.description: Use when the task is specifically Neki, PlanetScale’s sharded Postgres architecture: shard-key design, distributed Postgres tradeoffs, routing constraints, and operational decisions for large-scale Postgres workloads.

skills.name: nestjs-expert
skills.description: Use when building NestJS services that need strong module boundaries, dependency injection, DTO validation, guards or interceptors, auth boundaries, transport-aware module design, and production-safe TypeScript backend structure.

skills.name: nextjs-developer
skills.description: Use for implementing and reviewing Next.js App Router features, React Server Components and Actions, caching strategy, SEO, and production runtime behavior.

skills.name: nodejs-best-practices
skills.description: Use for modern Node.js backend architecture, runtime choices, worker or queue boundaries, edge-vs-server tradeoffs, reliability controls, and production-safe service implementation in the current LTS era.

skills.name: observability
skills.description: Use when designing or reviewing logging, monitoring, tracing, and alerting for production services. Covers structured logging, distributed tracing, metric collection, dashboard design, alert hygiene, SLO definition, and incident readiness across application and infrastructure layers.

skills.name: openai-docs
skills.description: Use when the task depends on current OpenAI platform or product documentation: API usage, model behavior, tool features, SDK guidance, or version-sensitive OpenAI docs that should be verified from official sources.

skills.name: performance-profiling
skills.description: Use when measuring, analyzing, and optimizing application performance with Core Web Vitals, bundle analysis, runtime profiling, and lighthouse audits.

skills.name: php-pro
skills.description: Use for modern PHP 8.4-era backend and application engineering with strict typing, framework-aware architecture, testing, and operational safety.

skills.name: playwright-e2e
skills.description: Use when writing or reviewing browser end-to-end tests with Playwright, debugging flaky UI automation, validating auth or checkout flows, or tightening CI evidence with traces and web-first assertions.

skills.name: postgres
skills.description: Use when the task is specifically PostgreSQL: schema design with Postgres features, query plans, indexes, JSONB, extensions, partitioning, logical replication, or managed Postgres operational tradeoffs.

skills.name: prompt-engineer
skills.description: Use when a prompt, instruction set, or agent/system message needs quality review: ambiguity, missing format constraints, unsafe assumptions, injection exposure, weak trigger wording, or brittle task framing.

skills.name: python-pro
skills.description: Use for production Python with 3.14-era typing, async, packaging, and testing standards. Use when building Python backend services, migrating legacy Python to typed architecture, implementing async I/O, or setting up pytest and packaging workflows.

skills.name: react-best-practices
skills.description: Use for performance-focused React and Next.js optimization audits, rerender analysis, bundle reduction, and server-rendering efficiency reviews.

skills.name: react-expert
skills.description: Use for framework-agnostic React architecture with React 19 patterns, state design, component boundaries, and rendering-performance decisions.

skills.name: redis
skills.description: Use when the task is specifically Redis: cache design, key strategy, TTLs, queues, rate limiting, pub/sub, memory tradeoffs, and safe persistence or eviction decisions.

skills.name: ruby-pro
skills.description: Use for modern Ruby 3.4-era backend, scripting, and application engineering with clear object boundaries, concurrency awareness, and test discipline.

skills.name: rust-pro
skills.description: Use for modern Rust systems/services with stable 1.94-era patterns, async correctness, and performance-focused design.

skills.name: seo-fundamentals
skills.description: Use when implementing technical SEO, content optimization, schema markup, or Core Web Vitals improvements for search engine visibility.

skills.name: skill-creator
skills.description: Create, test, improve, and package portable AI skills using the Agent Skills SKILL.md format across Claude Code, Codex, and GitHub Copilot, with Gemini conversion when needed. Use this when the user wants to build, repair, benchmark, or iterate on a reusable skill package.

skills.name: spec-miner
skills.description: Use when reverse-engineering legacy or undocumented systems into structured specifications with code-grounded evidence and EARS-format requirements.

skills.name: sqlite
skills.description: Use when the task is specifically SQLite or libSQL/Turso-style SQLite: local-first schema design, WAL/concurrency tradeoffs, embedded deployments, and lightweight migration strategy.

skills.name: supabase
skills.description: Use when the task is specifically Supabase: managed Postgres with RLS, auth, storage, edge functions, branching, and product decisions that depend on Supabase platform behavior.

skills.name: swift-pro
skills.description: Use for modern Swift 6.1-era application and systems engineering with strong concurrency, protocol boundaries, package structure, and Apple-platform correctness.

skills.name: tailwind-patterns
skills.description: Use for Tailwind CSS v4 architecture, CSS-first theme setup, utility composition, container-query usage, design-token hygiene, and dark-mode strategy.

skills.name: typescript-pro
skills.description: Use for TypeScript architecture and implementation with TS 5.9+ production baselines and TS 6/7 transition readiness. Use when designing type-safe APIs, refactoring to strict TypeScript, reducing type regressions in monorepos, or preparing for TS 6/7 behavior shifts.

skills.name: vitess
skills.description: Use when the task is specifically Vitess or Vitess-backed MySQL platforms: shard-key design, vindexes, resharding, query-routing constraints, Online DDL, and operational tradeoffs for horizontally scaled MySQL.

skills.name: web-perf
skills.description: Use for measuring and improving web performance with Core Web Vitals, rendering-path analysis, bundle and network prioritization, and framework-aware delivery tradeoffs.

skills.name: webapp-testing
skills.description: Use when choosing test depth for a web product, balancing unit, integration, browser, accessibility, and contract checks, or reviewing whether a change has the right verification shape before release.

builtinTools.name: route_resolve
builtinTools.description: Resolve an explicit workflow command, explicit custom agent, compatibility alias, or free-text intent into one workflow/agent route before skill loading.

builtinTools.name: skill_list_categories
builtinTools.description: List all skill categories available in the vault. Returns category names and skill counts.

builtinTools.name: skill_browse_category
builtinTools.description: Browse skills within a specific category. Returns skill IDs and short descriptions.

builtinTools.name: skill_search
builtinTools.description: Search skills by keyword. Matches against skill IDs and descriptions.

builtinTools.name: skill_validate
builtinTools.description: Validate an exact skill ID before loading it. Returns alias metadata and discoverable reference markdown paths.

builtinTools.name: skill_get
builtinTools.description: Get full content of a specific skill by ID. Returns SKILL.md content and optionally direct referenced markdown files.

builtinTools.name: skill_get_reference
builtinTools.description: Get one validated markdown reference file for a skill by exact relative path.

builtinTools.name: skill_budget_report
builtinTools.description: Report estimated context/token budget for selected and loaded skills.

builtinTools.name: postman_get_mode
builtinTools.description: Get current Postman MCP mode from cbx_config.

builtinTools.name: postman_set_mode
builtinTools.description: Set Postman MCP mode in cbx_config.

builtinTools.name: postman_get_status
builtinTools.description: Get Postman integration status and active profile.

builtinTools.name: stitch_get_mode
builtinTools.description: Get Stitch MCP mode from cbx_config.

builtinTools.name: stitch_set_profile
builtinTools.description: Switch active Stitch profile in cbx_config.

builtinTools.name: stitch_get_status
builtinTools.description: Get Stitch integration status and active profile.
